claim: Every photo taken on a smartphone runs 20-25 AI models locally for capture, coloring, and processing - about 1000+ AI models run across all apps on modern phones
actionable: Understand that edge AI is already pervasive in consumer devices; optimize for on-device deployment when building AI applications
source_title: AI Dev 25 | Krishna Sridhar: Shifting Paradigms—The Move of AI's Center of Gravity to Edge Devices

claim: 60-billion parameter models can run on edge devices like phones, cars, and IoT devices, enabling real-time speech, text, video, and image processing
actionable: Don't assume cloud-only approaches are necessary; large models can now run on-device for real-time latency-sensitive applications
source_title: AI Dev 25 | Krishna Sridhar: Shifting Paradigms—The Move of AI's Center of Gravity to Edge Devices

claim: Three key reasons for on-device AI: (1) Latency requirements (collision detection needs <20ms), (2) Privacy (data stays on device), (3) Efficiency (free compute)
actionable: When deciding between cloud and edge deployment, evaluate against latency budgets, privacy requirements, and total cost of compute
source_title: AI Dev 25 | Krishna Sridhar: Shifting Paradigms—The Move of AI's Center of Gravity to Edge Devices

claim: Qualcomm AI Hub automates model compilation from PyTorch/ONNX to run on Snapdragon devices without manual optimization
actionable: Use Qualcomm AI Hub to compile and test models on real edge hardware in the cloud before deployment
source_title: AI Dev 25 | Krishna Sridhar: Shifting Paradigms—The Move of AI's Center of Gravity to Edge Devices

claim: Edge AI systems map models to multiple compute units - CPUs, GPUs, and NPUs (neural processing units) - automatically optimizing utilization
actionable: When deploying edge models, let compilation systems automatically choose between CPU/GPU/NPU rather than manually specifying
source_title: AI Dev 25 | Krishna Sridhar: Shifting Paradigms—The Move of AI's Center of Gravity to Edge Devices

claim: Qualcomm offers a fleet of cloud devices (phones, XR devices, automotive silicon) so developers can iterate without needing physical device procurement
actionable: Use Qualcomm AI Hub's cloud device fleet to test models across 40+ device SKUs before deploying to real hardware
source_title: AI Dev 25 | Krishna Sridhar: Shifting Paradigms—The Move of AI's Center of Gravity to Edge Devices

claim: Modern edge devices have NPUs (neural processing units) specialized for matrix multiplications and convolutions, enabling efficient AI inference
actionable: Design models with these specialized operations in mind; they'll run far more efficiently on edge NPUs than equivalent CPU/GPU code
source_title: AI Dev 25 | Krishna Sridhar: Shifting Paradigms—The Move of AI's Center of Gravity to Edge Devices
